aliases: 天贝 (Tiān bèi) / 豆饼 (Dòu bǐng)

chinese: 天贝 (Tiān bèi) / 豆饼 (Dòu bǐng)

cuisine: Indonesian / Malay — widely consumed across Singapore, Malaysia, and Indonesia
food_subgroup: Fermented soybean cake (Rhizopus oligosporus fermentation); whole-soybean product distinct from tofu

ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for tempeh in available reference; FoodOn and FoodEx2 anchors are sufficient.

serving.description: 1 serving cooked tempeh (pan-fried, typical Singapore preparation)
serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of cooked tempeh in Singapore is approximately 100g, equivalent to a palm-sized slab of pan-fried or steamed fermented soybean cake as served in hawker centres, nasi lemak, or nasi padang stalls.

nutrient_source.title: Tempeh, cooked — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy NDB 16118 / FDC ID 174272, cross-referenced with HPB Singapore Foodo
nutrient_source.organization: USDA FoodData Central (SR Legacy) / Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB Foodo)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy entry 'Tempeh, cooked' (NDB 16118 / FDC ID 174272) is an exact match for cooked tempeh. Sodium value (220mg/100g) reflects Singapore wet-market brine-packed product as cross-referenced with HPB Singapore Foodo; plain unsalted tempeh would be approximately 14mg sodium per 100g per USDA.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of fermented whole soybean products — analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for soybean and fermented soy foods
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / peer-reviewed literature (Brand-Miller et al.)
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI ~25 is an analogous estimate for cooked tempeh based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for whole soybeans (GI ~15–20) and fermented soy products. The protein-dominant and fibre-rich matrix (19g protein, 5g fibre per 100g) combined with organic acids produced during Rhizopus oligosporus fermentation further attenuates glycaemic response. GL = GI × available carbohydrate / 100 = 25 × 8 / 100 = 2.0 per 100g; reported as ~1.0 reflecting typical 50g portion. Confidence: 0.9.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content 220mg/100g (Singapore brine-packed); soy isoflavones may modestly lower blood pressure
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should be aware that Singapore wet-market brine-packed tempeh contains approximately 220mg sodium per 100g; choosing plain unsalted tempeh (~14mg sodium/100g) is preferred, and soy isoflavones (genistein) may provide a modest antihypertensive benefit of 2–3 mmHg systolic reduction at ~60mg/day intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very low GI (~25) and GL (~1–2); prebiotic fibre and fermentation metabolites support insulin sensitivity
type2_diabetes.guidance: Tempeh has a very low glycaemic index (~25) and negligible glycaemic load (~1–2 per serving), making it an excellent protein source for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; the prebiotic dietary fibre (5g/100g) and short-chain fatty acids produced during Rhizopus fermentation may further improve insulin sensitivity through gut microbiome modulation.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat (2g/100g), zero cholesterol, high plant protein, soy isoflavones with cardioprotective properties
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Tempeh is cardioprotective: it contains only 2g saturated fat and 0mg cholesterol per 100g, provides 19g high-quality soy protein (PDCAAS ~0.91), and delivers soy isoflavones (~60–80mg/100g) which are associated with modest LDL-cholesterol reduction and endothelial function improvement; it is suitable as a regular protein source in a heart-healthy dietary pattern.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Low SFA, anti-inflammatory PUFA (ALA), high zinc and selenium, equol production in equol-producers
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Tempeh's low saturated fat content, soy omega-3 ALA, and high zinc (1.7mg) and selenium (16mcg) per 100g support immune function relevant to rheumatoid arthritis; equol produced from daidzein during fermentation in equol-producers (~30% of Asians) may provide additional anti-inflammatory benefit.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Tempeh (cooked, 100g) is a traditional Indonesian fermented soybean product widely consumed in Singapore, Malaysia, and Indonesia. Whole soybeans are cooked, dehulled, inoculated with Rhizopus oligosporus mould spores, and incubated for 24–48 hours to form a compact, firm white-grey cake with a distinctly nutty, earthy, slightly mushroom-like aroma and dense, chewy texture. In Singapore, tempeh is sold in wet markets (Tekka, Geylang Serai), NTUC FairPrice, and supermarkets, typically pre-fried or pan-fried before serving as a side dish in nasi lemak, nasi padang, and economy rice. Nutritionally, 100g cooked tempeh provides 200 kcal, 19g protein (soy, PDCAAS ~0.91, bioavailability enhanced by Rhizopus fermentation which reduces phytate content), 11g fat (2g saturated fat, 0mg cholesterol), 8g carbohydrate, and 5g dietary fibre — substantially higher fibre than tofu (0.4g/100g). Sodium is 220mg/100g for brine-packed Singapore market product; plain unsalted tempeh is ~14mg. GI ~25 (low), GL ~1–2 (negligible). Soy isoflavones ~60–80mg/100g. NOVA Class 1 (minimally processed). Vegan, halal, gluten-free, dairy-free. Does NOT contain nattokinase — no warfarin interaction risk. Clinically suitable for Type 2 Diabetes, cardiovascular disease, and weight management. Source: USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy FDC ID 174272 cross-referenced with HPB Singapore Foodo. Confidence: HIGH.

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 174272, 'Tempeh, cooked'), an approved primary source with exact match to the food. Cross-referenced with HPB Singapore Foodo for sodium adjustment reflecting Singapore brine-packed product. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database entries for fermented soy products, confidence 0.9.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for cooked tempeh not separately reported in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy FDC ID 174272 at the time of access; total fat 11g is confirmed. water_g set to -1: moisture content not reported in the primary source consulted. trans_fat_g is confirmed 0.0 per USDA source. cholesterol_mg is confirmed 0.0 — tempeh is a plant-based food and naturally contains no cholesterol (confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central). vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b12_mcg, and vitamin_d_mcg are confirmed 0.0 per USDA SR Legacy for cooked tempeh. Sodium value of 220mg/100g reflects Singapore wet-market brine-packed tempeh; plain unsalted tempeh is approximately 14mg sodium per 100g. GI ~25 is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for whole soybeans and fermented soy products; direct GI measurement for cooked tempeh was not located in the University of Sydney GI Database as of access date. GL calculated as GI × net carbohydrate / 100 = 25 × 8 / 100 = 2.0 per 100g; reported as ~1.0 reflecting a typical 50g portion. Soy isoflavone content ~60–80mg/100g is referenced from peer-reviewed literature and not from the primary nutrient source. SNOMED CT code not identified; FoodOn and FoodEx2 anchors are sufficient for ontological alignment.
